skill.name: 2026-coach
skill.description: Executive coaching skill for planning 2026 with process goals, discovery questions, annual milestones, weekly checklists, and accountability artifacts.
skill.summary: Plan 2026 with process goals, annual strategy, weekly tracking, and AI coaching context.
skill.author: BayramAnnakov

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found they are Markdown examples, plain-language coaching text, DOI or GitHub links, and user-directed file paths. No executable code, hidden command execution, credential access, data exfiltration, or prompt-injection attempt was confirmed. The skill is safe to publish with a low-risk note because it asks an AI agent to create planning files in a user-selected location.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Are Not Runtime Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The external command detections are Markdown code fences, inline examples, directory trees, and installation instructions. There is no script file or skill logic that executes shell commands automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are documentation or code-fence content in README.md and SKILL.md. No executable implementation file or automatic command invocation is present in the scanned file set.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Documentation References
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The URL detections point to a DOI source, GitHub installation links, the author profile, and a companion skill recommendation. No evidence found that the skill sends user data to any network endpoint.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each cited URL is visible documentation or citation text. I found no fetch, curl, webhook, telemetry, or credential transfer behavior.

low_findings.title: Hidden Directory Paths Are Installation Guidance
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE with minor operational risk: The .claude paths describe where users can install the skill. The skill also asks where to create coaching files, so users should choose an expected directory before allowing file writes.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hidden-path evidence appears in installation instructions and the file creation workflow. It is user-directed file handling, not stealth access to hidden files.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Matches Are Plain Language
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The weak cryptography detections match words such as descriptions, Desktop, describing, provides, despite, and design. No evidence found of MD5, DES, SHA-1, or other weak cryptographic use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are prose, metadata, or coaching instructions. They do not contain cryptographic APIs, hashes, keys, encryption routines, or integrity checks.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Matches Are Coaching Prompts
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The system reconnaissance detections are questions and coaching prompts about progress, blockers, and learning. They do not inspect hostnames, users, processes, environment variables, or system configuration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are human coaching questions in templates and prompt examples. No commands or APIs for host reconnaissance are present.

content.value_statement: Annual goals often fail because they focus on outcomes users cannot fully control. This skill turns a 2026 vision into daily behaviors, weekly checklists, and review artifacts.
actual_capabilities: Guides a four-round discovery process for current state, vision, strategy, and daily process design.
Creates an annual goal hierarchy with one outcome goal and quarterly milestones.
Converts outcome goals into controllable daily and weekly process goals.
Uses included templates for an annual plan, coaching context, and weekly checklist.
Supports conversational use in Claude.ai and interactive use in Claude Code.
limitations: It is a coaching aid, not a licensed therapy, legal, financial, or medical service.
The research claim is cited by the skill and should be verified for critical decisions.
File creation depends on the host assistant and the directory permissions users grant.
Results depend on accurate user input and regular weekly review.

use_cases.title: Founder Annual Planning
use_cases.description: Define one 2026 business outcome, quarterly milestones, and daily growth behaviors that can be tracked each week.

use_cases.title: Executive Focus System
use_cases.description: Translate strategic priorities into process goals, review questions, and coaching context for repeated AI sessions.

use_cases.title: Creator Productivity Plan
use_cases.description: Turn publishing, sales, or learning goals into daily checklists and weekly reflection prompts.

prompt_templates.title: Start My 2026 Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner user who wants guided discovery.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use 2026-coach to help me plan my year. Start with discovery questions and wait for my answers before creating goals.

prompt_templates.title: Convert Goals to Behaviors
prompt_templates.scenario: User already has an annual goal.
prompt_templates.prompt: My 2026 outcome goal is [goal]. Convert it into quarterly milestones, weekly process goals, and daily behaviors I control.

prompt_templates.title: Create Tracking Artifacts
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants files for ongoing accountability.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the skill templates to create my annual plan, coaching context, and first weekly checklist in a coaching folder.

prompt_templates.title: Review and Recalibrate
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user with existing weekly results.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my completed week, identify patterns, adjust next week process goals, and update the coaching context for future sessions.

output_examples.input: I want to reach 100,000 monthly recurring revenue in 2026.
output_examples.output: The skill produces a north-star outcome, quarterly revenue milestones, daily outreach or product behaviors, weekly totals, and review prompts.

output_examples.input: I have too many priorities and need a weekly plan.
output_examples.output: The skill identifies one primary metric, selects the highest-leverage behaviors, and creates a week-one checklist with reflection questions.

output_examples.input: I want ongoing AI coaching after the plan is created.
output_examples.output: The skill creates a coaching context artifact with profile details, process targets, risks, review questions, and prompt guidance.

best_practices: Answer discovery questions with real constraints, available time, and current baseline metrics.
Choose process goals that can be completed without relying on another person or external outcome.
Run a weekly review before changing targets, so changes are based on observed behavior.
anti_patterns: Do not create many annual goals before selecting one primary metric.
Do not treat outcome targets as daily tasks, because outcomes are not fully controllable.
Do not allow file creation in an unexpected directory or without reviewing the target path.

faq.question: Does this skill require Claude Code?
faq.answer: No. It supports Claude Code, Claude.ai, and other assistants that can follow the skill prompt.

faq.question: What files can it create?
faq.answer: It can create an annual plan, coaching context, and weekly checklist using the included Markdown templates.

faq.question: Is this a business coaching replacement?
faq.answer: No. It is a structured planning aid and accountability prompt system, not professional advisory services.

faq.question: Can I use it for personal goals?
faq.answer: Yes. The process goal method can apply to fitness, writing, learning, and career goals.

faq.question: Why does the audit mention filesystem risk?
faq.answer: The skill asks where to create planning files. Users should confirm the target directory before writing files.

faq.question: Does it send my data to external services?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The reviewed files contain links and citations, but no network request code.
